rationale: At pinned SHA 4cbf437b, Fix 1 fully specifies and grounds BranchExistsE's present/absent/infra-error contract, adds the anti-false-refusal falsifier, two existence-discriminated exact-named tests, and mixed-list wrapper parity. Fix 2 changes AC1 to exact-run TestScanOrphanedClosedBeads_ErrorPreserving while retaining the package no-regression run. I audited AC1-AC7: every named test is paired with an exact-named -run. In an absolute /tmp archive, all 14 new named tests were absent (grep exit 1), including TestBranchExistsE_ThreeWay and TestApproveImpl_DeletedBranchNoRefusal; BranchExistsE and ScanOrphanedClosedBeads had zero internal Go-code hits; the AC4 anchor was absent; and every complete AC1-AC7 proof chain exited 1. The round diff contains only spec.md and panel artifacts, all four OQs remain resolved, and no new defect was found.

findings.note: R1, OQ2, and Scope state the three-way BranchExistsE contract and accurately cite the RevParseRef/ErrRefNotFound exit-1-versus-128 precedent.
R1's falsified-if clause now rejects any genuinely absent branch result other than (false, nil), pinning the deleted-branch happy path.
AC1 adds an existence-discriminated exact-named TestBranchExistsE_ThreeWay proof; its existence grep exits 1 at 4cbf437b.
AC2 adds an existence-discriminated exact-named TestApproveImpl_DeletedBranchNoRefusal proof; its existence grep exits 1 at 4cbf437b.
AC1 requires a mixed closed-bead list where the core errors on one bead while the fail-open wrapper still returns later provable orphans byte-identically.
AC1 now chains the lifecycle existence grep to go test ./internal/lifecycle -run 'TestScanOrphanedClosedBeads_ErrorPreserving' -v; all AC1-AC7 named tests have exact-named runs.
